title: Build AI-assisted PlatformIO-to-Zephyr board intake tool
description:
## Why
Meshtastic and MeshCore encode a large amount of useful board knowledge in PlatformIO variants and Arduino-oriented source, but there is no trustworthy turnkey transformer into Zephyr board definitions. AI can reduce the manual porting load by extracting facts, generating reviewable drafts, and flagging uncertainty, while humans keep final ownership of Zephyr board semantics and hardware validation.

## Scope
Create a tool-assisted workflow that ingests PlatformIO board JSON, platformio.ini environments, Meshtastic/MeshCore variant headers, and known source patterns, then emits a structured board intake report for each candidate LICHEN target. The report should include MCU/SoC, radio chip, display controller, buttons, LEDs, GNSS, RTC/time, PMIC/battery, storage, BLE/serial transports, SPI/I2C/UART buses, pin mappings, Zephyr upstream board status, suggested Zephyr board/overlay files to create or reuse, LICHEN capability symbols, confidence levels, and unresolved questions.

## Acceptance Criteria
The tool produces deterministic, reviewable outputs with source references for every extracted fact. It never claims generated Zephyr board support is valid without build and smoke-test evidence. It can process at least one Meshtastic variant and one MeshCore variant into useful Zephyr port checklists. It cleanly separates parsed facts, AI inferences, and human decisions. Follow-up Beads are created for any generated board port work. Two review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Implementation evidence (2026-07-01):
- Added Python board intake CLI/module at python/src/lichen/board_intake.py and script entry lichen-board-intake in python/pyproject.toml.
- Tool ingests PlatformIO board JSON, platformio.ini envs, inherited envs via extends, multiline build_flags, -D build flag facts, and variant header #define facts.
- Report output separates source_set, parsed_facts, inferred_suggestions, human_decisions, unresolved_questions, zephyr_port_checklist, follow_up_beads, and zephyr_support_status.
- Every parsed fact has source refs with path, line_start, line_end, and extract. Inferences remain separate and include source refs. human_decisions is empty by default.
- Zephyr support is never claimed as valid; zephyr_support_status and checklist entries remain needs_validation until separate build/smoke evidence exists.
- follow_up_beads are proposed report records only; the CLI does not call bd. No real generated board-port follow-up Beads were created because the included inputs are synthetic fixtures, not selected production targets.
- Tests cover Meshtastic-like and MeshCore-like fixture inputs, inherited PlatformIO envs, deterministic CLI output, no validated support claims, radio/MCU/variant/peripheral conflicts, missing scope facts, duplicate IDs, and source refs.

Review evidence:
- Test-design explorer Popper recommended package/test placement and acceptance tests: schema sections, source refs per fact, parsed/inferred/human separation, deterministic output, Meshtastic-like and MeshCore-like fixtures, no unearned support claims, conflict questions, and proposed follow-up Beads. Implemented the core recommendations in python/src/lichen/board_intake.py and python/tests/test_board_intake.py.
- First code review round: Boyle found P2s for synthetic empty frameworks observed fact, wrong source refs across repeated PlatformIO env keys, and missing PlatformIO extends inheritance. Socrates found P2s for weak multiline build_flags source refs and too-narrow missing/conflicting fact reporting. Fixed by suppressing absent frameworks facts, preserving PlatformIO section/value source spans, resolving extends, parsing multiline values, and adding broader missing scope questions.
- Second review round: Lorentz and Harvey both found remaining P2 that MCU/variant conflicts were not detected. Fixed by adding value conflict detection for MCU/SoC and PlatformIO variant plus regression tests.
- Third review round: Beauvoir found remaining P2 for same-key pin/bus/peripheral conflicts beyond radio/MCU/variant. Fixed by parsing PlatformIO -D flags as platformio_define facts and adding same-key peripheral conflict detection plus PIN_SCK/DISPLAY_MODEL regression tests.
- Final post-fix reviews: Darwin and Nash reported no P0/P1/P2; closure acceptable. Darwin verified 8-test suite and same-key peripheral conflict coverage. Nash verified source-backed facts, inheritance/multiline refs, unresolved questions, deterministic output, no Zephyr validation claims, and tests.

Test/check evidence:
- python3 -m pytest python/tests/test_board_intake.py: PASS, 8 passed in 0.03s.
- python3 -m ruff check python/src/lichen/board_intake.py python/tests/test_board_intake.py: PASS, All checks passed.
- python3 -m compileall -q python/src/lichen/board_intake.py python/tests/test_board_intake.py: PASS.
- git diff --check: PASS.
- CLI smoke with inherited env and multiline -D flags: PASS; deterministic JSON via cmp and report kept zephyr_support_status=needs_validation.
- CLI smoke with conflicting PIN_SCK sources: PASS; emitted question.peripheral.pin-sck.conflict and kept zephyr_support_status=needs_validation.
- No EC2 required; Python/tooling-only work ran locally.
close_reason: Completed deterministic Python board intake CLI/report tool with source-backed facts, separated inferences/decisions, no Zephyr validation claims, Meshtastic-like and MeshCore-like tests, conflict reporting, review evidence, and local checks.
